subject: logic
title: The Odd One Out
question: Which is the odd one out: dog, cat, whale, bat, snake? I want you to give me as many different answers as you can, each with a different reason.
modelReasoningPath: What to listen for: Does the child generate multiple answers without being asked twice? Can they articulate a principle rather than just an observation ("whale is different because... it lives in water")? A strong child will offer several unprompted and find creative distinctions. If a child gets stuck after one answer, ask: "Can you think of a reason a completely different animal could be the odd one out?" Adaptability here means shifting the lens, not just the answer. --- ## SECTION B - Lateral Thinking Scenarios Puzzles where the most obvious interpretation is wrong. The child must question their assumptions. Interviewer note: For all scenarios in this section, the child should ask yes/no questions if they wish, or reason aloud. The goal is to watch them dismantle their initial assumption. ---
